examples.title: Algorithm: LinearLayout Child Arrangement
examples.description: Demonstrates how LinearLayout positions child views sequentially in a single direction
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ArrangeChildrenLinear
BEGIN
 SET orientation to horizontal or vertical
 FOR each child IN children
 MEASURE child's width and height
 IF orientation is horizontal
 ALLOCATE width based on weight_sum and layout_weight
 POSITION child next to previous (left to right)
 ELSE
 ALLOCATE height based on weight_sum and layout_weight
 POSITION child below previous (top to bottom)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation:
1. Orientation determines direction (horizontal/vertical)
2. Each child is measured considering parent's constraints
3. Weight attributes distribute remaining space proportionally
4. Children positioned sequentially without overlapping

examples.title: Algorithm: AbsoluteLayout Positioning
examples.description: Positions child views using exact X/Y coordinates relative to parent
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PositionChildrenAbsolute
BEGIN
 FOR each child IN children
 GET x_coordinate and y_coordinate from layout parameters
 SET child's position to (x_coordinate, y_coordinate)
 MEASURE child with exact dimensions if specified
 DRAW child at specified coordinates (may overlap others)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation:
1. Each child's position is defined explicitly
2. No automatic spacing or arrangement
3. Overlapping occurs if coordinates overlap
4. Rarely used due to lack of responsiveness

examples.title: Algorithm: FrameLayout Stacking
examples.description: Stacks child views on top of each other in Z-order
examples.code:
ALGORITHM StackChildrenFrame
BEGIN
 SET parent dimensions
 FOR each child IN children
 MEASURE child to match parent or specified size
 POSITION child at (0,0) by default
 DRAW child in order (last child on top)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation:
1. All children are positioned at top-left corner
2. Later children draw over previous ones
3. Useful for overlays and single-view containers
4. Manages visibility through Z-ordering
